county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Warren County formed 1846 from Polk. County seat is Indianola.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Indianola holds deeds from 1849; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Warren County is centered on Indianola, home of Simpson College (est. 1860, Methodist). George Washington Carver attended Simpson before transferring to Iowa State. National Balloon Classic held in Indianola. Warren County Historical Society. Hartford, Milo, and Norwalk communities. Middle and South Rivers. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Warren County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.source_name: Warren County Deed Records -- County Recorder
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Indianola
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books, mortgages, and land records from 1846 to present. County Recorder maintains grantor/grantee indexes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.source_name: Warren County Probate Records -- Clerk of District Court
source_profiles.repository: Clerk of District Court, Indianola
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1846. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.source_name: Warren County Marriage Records
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Indianola / Iowa Department of Public Health
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1846. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Warren County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents (homesteads, cash sales, military warrants) for Warren County. Iowa is a public land state; most land was transferred from federal ownership between 1838 and 1870.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Federal records complete; originals at NARA

source_profiles.source_name: Warren County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1850-1950 for Warren County. Iowa territorial census 1836, 1838; Iowa state census 1847, 1849, 1851-1925 (every 2 years). Agricultural schedules available.

source_profiles.source_name: Warren County Cemetery Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa Genealogical Society / Find A Grave / Warren County Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Warren County. Township cemeteries, church cemeteries, and city cemeteries. Iowa Genealogical Society and county historical societies have published transcription volumes. Find A Grave has extensive Iowa coverage.

source_profiles.source_name: Warren County Tax Lists and Assessment Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / Warren County Auditor, Indianola
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists, personal property assessments, and plat books for Warren County. Tax lists name property owners and describe holdings. Iowa State Archives holds some transferred tax records on microfilm.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Washington County formed 1837 from Dubuque (Indian Cession). County seat is Washington.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Washington holds deeds from 1839; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Washington County was an early settlement area with strong ties to the Quaker Underground Railroad network. Crooked Creek Friends Meeting records. Washington County Historical Society. Wellman, Kalona (Amish/Mennonite community -- some of the largest in the Midwest), and Riverside communities. The Amish and Mennonite settlements around Kalona have church and community records at the Mennonite Historical Library (Goshen, IN) and local archives. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Washington County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1837. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1837. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

source_profiles.repository: Iowa Yearly Meeting / Swarthmore College Friends Historical Library / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Friends meeting records including minutes, membership lists, certificates of removal, birth/death/marriage registers. Washington County Quaker meetings were active in the Underground Railroad.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Wayne County formed 1846 from Appanoose. County seat is Corydon.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Corydon holds deeds from 1851; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Wayne County in southern Iowa was settled by migrants from Missouri and the border South. The county saw Civil War skirmishing near the Missouri border. Corydon was where the Jesse James gang robbed the bank in 1871. Wayne County Historical Society. Seymour, Allerton, and Humeston communities. Chariton River headwaters. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Wayne County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.parent_county: Risley (renamed; Yell renamed Risley, then Webster)
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Webster County formed 1851 from Risley (renamed; Yell renamed Risley, then Webster). County seat is Fort Dodge.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Fort Dodge holds deeds from 1856; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Webster County is centered on Fort Dodge, named for the military post established in 1850. Fort Dodge was a major gypsum mining center -- mining company records. The Fort Dodge historical gypsum quarries produced the Cardiff Giant (famous 1869 hoax). Webster County Historical Society and Fort Museum. Blanden Memorial Art Museum. Lehigh coal mining community. Badger and Dayton communities. Des Moines River. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Webster County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1851. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1851. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Winnebago County formed 1851 from Indian lands. County seat is Forest City.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Forest City holds deeds from 1858; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Winnebago County was settled in the 1870s-1880s by Norwegian immigrants. Forest City is home to Waldorf University (formerly Waldorf College, Norwegian Lutheran, est. 1903). Winnebago Industries (motorhomes) founded here. Winnebago County Historical Society. Lake Mills and Buffalo Center communities. Lime Creek (now Winnebago River). Norwegian Lutheran church records essential. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Winnebago County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Winneshiek County formed 1847 from Indian lands. County seat is Decorah.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Decorah holds deeds from 1851; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Winneshiek County is the heart of Norwegian Iowa. Decorah is home to Luther College (est. 1861, Norwegian Lutheran) and the Vesterheim Norwegian-American Museum, the most comprehensive Norwegian-American museum in the US. Vesterheim holds church records, immigrant letters, ship passenger lists, and genealogical files. Norwegian-American Historical Association connections. Winneshiek County Historical Society. Fort Atkinson (established to protect Winnebago Indians). Upper Iowa River. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Winneshiek County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Woodbury County formed 1851 from Indian lands. County seat is Sioux City.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Sioux City holds deeds from 1854; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Woodbury County is centered on Sioux City, Iowa's fourth largest city at the junction of the Big Sioux and Missouri Rivers. Major meatpacking center (Armour, Swift, Cudahy) -- employment records document diverse worker communities. Sioux City Public Museum. Sergeant Floyd Monument (Lewis and Clark expedition). Woodbury County Historical Society. Salix and Correctionville communities. Morningside College (now Morningside University) archives. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Woodbury County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Worth County formed 1851 from Mitchell. County seat is Northwood.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Northwood holds deeds from 1857; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Worth County on the Minnesota border was settled by Scandinavian (predominantly Norwegian) and German immigrants in the 1860s-1870s. Northwood is the county seat. Worth County Historical Society. Manly, Grafton, and Fertile communities. Shell Rock River headwaters. Norwegian Lutheran church records. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Worth County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Wright County formed 1851 from Webster. County seat is Clarion.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Clarion holds deeds from 1856; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Wright County was settled in the 1870s. Clarion and Eagle Grove are the primary communities. Eagle Grove was a railroad junction. Wright County Historical Society. Belmond and Goldfield communities. Boone River and Iowa River headwaters. Dows and Rowan. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Wright County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.source_name: Wright County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents (homesteads, cash sales, military warrants) for Wright County. Iowa is a public land state; most land was transferred from federal ownership between 1838 and 1870.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Federal records complete; originals at NARA

source_profiles.source_name: Wright County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1860-1950 for Wright County. Iowa territorial census 1836, 1838; Iowa state census 1847, 1849, 1851-1925 (every 2 years). Agricultural schedules available.

source_profiles.source_name: Wright County Cemetery Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa Genealogical Society / Find A Grave / Wright County Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Wright County. Township cemeteries, church cemeteries, and city cemeteries. Iowa Genealogical Society and county historical societies have published transcription volumes. Find A Grave has extensive Iowa coverage.

source_profiles.source_name: Wright County Tax Lists and Assessment Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / Wright County Auditor, Clarion
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists, personal property assessments, and plat books for Wright County. Tax lists name property owners and describe holdings. Iowa State Archives holds some transferred tax records on microfilm.

source_profiles.source_name: Wright County Scandinavian Church Records -- Lutheran Congregations
source_profiles.repository: Luther College Archives, Decorah / ELCA Archives / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Norwegian and Swedish Lutheran church records for Wright County congregations. Baptisms, confirmations, marriages, burials. Many Norwegian Synod and Hauge Synod congregations.
